_comment: Material default-activation contract (Windows).
The defect this pins: the entire Material widget treatment ships in every MSI
but is dormant, because upstream keeps it behind two environment variables that
no product code sets. vcl/source/gdi/salgdilayout.cxx enables the file-defined
widget-draw backend (FileDefinitionWidgetDraw) only when VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE
is set, and the theme-name guards select the shared theme only when
VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME == 'material'. The Material assets themselves DO ship
(vcl/Package_theme_definitions.mk installs material/definition.xml).
The fix, owned by this contract: desktop/source/app/sofficemain.cxx defaults both
variables ON at the very top of soffice_main(), before any consumer in the process
reads them, under a #ifdef _WIN32 guard (this is the Windows-only rewrite; the Linux
CI leg and the CppunitTests never enter soffice_main and stay stock). Fail-closed
contract semantics:
(A) Full opt-out -- LIBREOFFICE_MATERIAL_THEME=off (or =0), case-insensitive --
does nothing at all and leaves the process environment untouched.
(B) User override wins -- if VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME is already set, it is never
overwritten; VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE is only defaulted to '1' when it is
unset AND the pre-set theme is non-empty.
(C) Otherwise VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME is set to 'material' and, if unset,
VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE is set to '1'. Plain C runtime only (getenv /
_putenv_s / _stricmp); this runs before framework/UNO/VCL init.
The checker (bin/check-material-default-activation.py) cross-validates every anchor
below against real, comment-stripped source, and additionally proves the two assets
the activation depends on are still present -- salgdilayout.cxx still gates on
VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE and Package_theme_definitions.mk still ships
material/definition.xml -- so activation cannot outlive its assets.
Source + wiring evidence only: runtime_verified is false throughout. No native
build, no dialog/theme pixels, and no runtime observation of the activated theme is
claimed. First real visual verification is a specified carveout pending a built MSI.
design_reference: README.md (manual VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE / VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME opt-in that this fork now defaults on), docs/design/00-windows-rewrite-contract.md (Windows-only rewrite boundary)

activation.note: The whole block sits inside soffice_main() before first_statement_anchor, guarded by #ifdef _WIN32. The opt-out token, the respect-existing getenv check, and both _putenv_s calls must all appear in that pre-first-statement region. A moved block, a dropped guard, a dropped opt-out, a dropped override-respect, or a drifted _putenv_s value fails closed.

gate.note: SalGraphics::initWidgetDrawBackends still gates FileDefinitionWidgetDraw on VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE; the default-on wiring targets exactly this switch.

assets.note: The Material theme definition the 'material' theme name resolves to still ships; activation cannot outlive its assets.

first_visual_verification.note: This host has no build root, so no MSI exists to launch: that the default-on wiring actually renders Material widgets in a shipped install is specified only, pending a real build. runtime_verified stays false until an installed binary is observed.
